title: I am already enough. I have always been.
subtitle: An affirmation for the core belief that worth is conditional
excerpt: For the moment when the not-enough feeling arrives, not about a specific failure but as a ground state. A two-part affirmation for the belief that worth is something to be earned rather than something already present.

practice.whenToUse: When the not-enough feeling is running as a background state rather than pointing at a specific failure

content.text: The belief that worth has to be earned, that you are enough once you have achieved enough, lost enough weight, been a good enough mother, made enough money, is one of the more persistent sources of low-grade distress. The condition keeps moving. This affirmation pushes back against the condition itself.
Say this three times, slowly, hand on heart:
I am already enough. I have always been.
The second sentence matters. Not just now, after the work done or the progress made. Always. The enoughness is not new information. It was true before the achievement and will be true after.
